facility_name: Google Council Bluffs / Papillion / Omaha TPU Cluster
opportunity_summary: Three Google data center campuses within ~15 miles of each other (Council Bluffs, Omaha, Papillion) plus Lincoln ~50 miles away. Combined capacity north of 500MW in 2023, large portion allocated to TPUs. Google has deployed liquid cooling at GW scale across 2000+ TPU pods with 99.999% uptime since 2018. Papillion adds >250MW. All four campuses forming GW-scale AI training cluster by 2026. Liquid cooling is standard for all TPU racks.
notes: Google Hot Chips 2025: liquid cooling deployed since TPU v3 (2018). Split-flow cold plates, rack-level CDUs, ~1GW total liquid cooling capacity. SemiAnalysis reports this Midwest cluster is one of the largest TPU training sites globally. Papillion confirmed as $600M+ investment. Source: Hot Chips Aug 2025, SemiAnalysis 2025, DCD 2023.

facility_name: Google New Albany Data Center
opportunity_summary: Google operates coolant distribution units at New Albany, Ohio campus. Part of Google's GW-scale liquid-cooled TPU deployment across 2000+ pods globally. Google's Project Deschutes CDU is 5th-gen design contributed to OCP. Ohio is a major Google cloud region with TPU infrastructure.
notes: Google's CDU infrastructure confirmed at New Albany. Google's standard for all TPU racks (v3 onward) is liquid cooling with split-flow cold plates. Specific MW capacity not disclosed. Source: Baxtel facility listing, Hot Chips 2025 presentation.

facility_name: Google Wilbarger County Texas Data Center
opportunity_summary: Google's $40B Texas investment. Advanced air-cooling technology limiting water consumption to critical campus operations only. Partnership with AES for clean energy. Water-scarce region drove decision for air cooling over evaporative/liquid. However, Google's TPU racks still require liquid cooling internally -- advanced air is for facility-level heat rejection.
notes: Google chose advanced air cooling for facility heat rejection to avoid water use in drought-prone Texas. But ALL Google TPU pods use liquid cooling at rack level regardless of facility-level cooling approach. Monitor for hybrid deployment. Source: New Atlas 2025, IT Pro 2025.

facility_name: Google Mayes County Oklahoma Data Center Expansion
opportunity_summary: $9B Google investment in Oklahoma AI/cloud infrastructure. One of Google's longest-running US data center locations (since 2007). Google has deployed liquid cooling at GW scale across 2000+ TPU pods globally; Oklahoma campus almost certainly hosts liquid-cooled TPU racks as part of this fleet.
notes: Google confirmed $9B Oklahoma investment. Google liquid cools all TPU pods since v3 (2018). While facility-level cooling specifics for Pryor Creek not individually disclosed, Google's global TPU fleet of 2000+ pods is all liquid cooled. Source: Google DC page, Hot Chips 2025.

facility_name: Google Germany Data Center (Hanau / Frankfurt Region)
opportunity_summary: Part of EUR 5.5B German expansion. Hanau currently uses air pressure and ambient air cooling per Google's official page. However, Google's German expansion is described as featuring modular liquid-cooled designs for highest-density AI workloads. Europe is where Google is deploying liquid cooling most aggressively outside US. Monitor for liquid cooling retrofit/expansion.
notes: Hanau facility currently documented as air cooled. But Google's German expansion program (EUR 5.5B) specifically includes liquid-cooled designs for AI. Google demonstrated 1MW liquid-cooled racks at OCP EMEA. Source: Google DC page, EnkiAI 2025.

facility_name: Microsoft Fairwater Atlanta AI Datacenter
opportunity_summary: Second Fairwater site operational since Oct/Nov 2025. Connected to Wisconsin Fairwater via AI WAN dedicated fiber. Two-story design, no UPS or gen-sets (revolutionary DC design). Closed-loop liquid cooling, 140kW per rack, 1,360kW per row. Part of Microsoft's first AI superfactory network.
notes: Launched Nov 2025. No traditional UPS or generator sets -- uses modular power approach. Connected to Wisconsin Fairwater with dedicated fiber AI WAN. Closed-loop non-evaporative liquid cooling. Source: DCD Nov 2025, Microsoft Blog Nov 2025.

facility_name: Microsoft Fairwater Loughton UK (nScale Partnership)
opportunity_summary: Part of Microsoft's $30B UK investment through 2028. UK's largest supercomputer. 23,000+ NVIDIA GPUs. Partnership with nScale. Fairwater blueprint = closed-loop liquid cooling. Part of distributed AI superfactory network.
notes: Announced as part of Fairwater global rollout. nScale partnership for UK's largest AI supercomputer. Fairwater design standard = closed-loop non-evaporative liquid cooling at 140kW/rack. Source: DCF 2025, Microsoft Blog Nov 2025.

facility_name: Microsoft Narvik Norway AI Datacenter (nScale/Aker JV)
opportunity_summary: $6.2B investment. Hydropower-backed AI campus. Abundant renewable energy and natural cooling from Norway's northern climate. Fairwater blueprint = closed-loop liquid cooling. Initial services expected online 2026. Partnership with nScale and Aker.
notes: NOTE: We already have a Microsoft Narvik entry in eu-facilities.json but with limited detail. This adds Fairwater blueprint confirmation, $6.2B figure, nScale/Aker JV detail, and 2026 timeline. Source: DCF 2025.

facility_name: Microsoft West Des Moines Ginger East (Zero-Water Cooling)
opportunity_summary: 5th and final building at 124-acre Ginger East campus (1475 SE Maffitt Lake Road). New zero-water cooling technology -- closed-loop DLC with no evaporation. Part of Microsoft's global transition to zero-water cooling starting Aug 2024. Iowa is major Microsoft campus where previous buildings used 48.7M gallons evaporative cooling in 2025.
notes: Council approved site plan modification. Zero-water liquid cooling = closed-loop DLC that saves 125M+ liters/year vs evaporative. Previous Iowa buildings still use evaporative; this is the retrofit/upgrade generation. Source: Axios Jan 2026, HPCwire Jan 2025.

facility_name: Microsoft Castroville Texas Data Center (SAT 82)
opportunity_summary: $400M investment. 195,670 sq ft single-story DC at 2580 FM Road 471 N. Construction mid-Aug 2026, completion June 2028. Part of Microsoft's San Antonio metro expansion. All new MS DCs from Aug 2024 onward designed with zero-water closed-loop liquid cooling.
notes: Microsoft has multiple data centers in San Antonio region. Castroville is west of SA. All post-Aug-2024 MS designs = closed-loop zero-water liquid cooling by default. Source: DCD, MS Cloud Blog Dec 2024.

facility_name: Microsoft Levis Quebec Data Center
opportunity_summary: Phase 1 construction began April 2024 in Charny neighborhood. Part of 4-DC Quebec City expansion (Saint-Augustin-de-Desmaures, Donnaconna, L'Ancienne-Lorette, Levis). All post-Aug-2024 MS designs use closed-loop zero-water liquid cooling. Hydropower-rich Quebec grid.
notes: Four Microsoft DCs near Quebec City. Construction started 2024. All new MS designs from Aug 2024 = zero-water closed-loop liquid cooling default. Source: Microsoft Local blog, CoStar.

facility_name: AWS Project Rainier — St. Joseph County, Indiana
opportunity_summary: World's largest known deployment of non-NVIDIA compute. $11B+ investment. 500,000+ AWS Trainium 2 chips. 30 buildings each 200,000 sq ft. Direct-to-chip liquid cooling with closed-loop system confirmed. Uses outside air for facility cooling in colder months. Fully operational Oct 2025, built in under 1 year. Built for Anthropic AI training.
notes: Operational Oct 2025. 500,000 Trainium 2 chips, NOT NVIDIA GPUs. Largest non-NVIDIA AI compute cluster anywhere. Direct-to-chip liquid plus outside air hybrid. $15B total Indiana investment announced. Source: About Amazon Oct 2025, DCK Oct 2025.

facility_name: AWS Northern Virginia Mattermeade Campus
opportunity_summary: 1,143 acres across 13 parcels. 11 two-story DC buildings plus office facility. 770MW+ operational capacity target by 2027. AWS's IRHX in-house liquid cooling system (rack-level DLC) is retrofittable into existing and new DCs. AWS goal: 20%+ ML capacity liquid-cooled by 2026. Flagship Virginia facilities among most likely IRHX deployment sites.
notes: AWS IRHX unveiled Jul 2025: CDU + integrated pumping unit + in-row fan-coil modules. Reduces mechanical energy 46%. Co-engineered with NVIDIA in 11 months. Fits existing rack dimensions. AWS target 20%+ ML liquid cooled by 2026. Source: Black Ridge 2026, Dell'Oro Jul 2025.

facility_name: AWS Mississippi Data Center Complex
opportunity_summary: 500MW capacity supplied by Mississippi Power Company. Construction began late 2025, completion 2033. Multi-phase buildout. AWS IRHX liquid cooling is default for new high-density builds. At 500MW scale, liquid cooling is almost certain for AI workloads.
notes: Liquid cooling not individually confirmed for this specific site, but AWS IRHX is the standard for all new AI infrastructure. 500MW = high-density AI tier. Source: Black Ridge 2026.

facility_name: AWS Ohio Sunbury Campus
opportunity_summary: $2B data center in Central Ohio. Construction began late 2025, completion by 2033. AWS IRHX liquid cooling is standard for new AI builds. AWS target: 20%+ ML capacity liquid cooled by 2026.
notes: Liquid cooling not individually confirmed for Sunbury but AWS's IRHX is the standard for all new high-density builds. Source: Black Ridge 2026.

facility_name: Meta Gallatin Data Center — Gallatin, TN (AALC Retrofit)
opportunity_summary: Meta committed to company-wide shift to liquid cooling. By early 2025, Meta is deploying AI servers with AALC (Air-Assisted Liquid Cooling) for direct-to-chip cooling across its fleet of existing data centers. Gallatin ($1.5B+) is a major Meta campus in Tennessee. AALC uses closed-loop rear-door heat exchangers with cold plates -- retrofittable into existing 20kW air-cooled halls to achieve 120kW/rack.
notes: Meta's AALC approach: cold plates on chips + rear-door heat exchanger + reservoir & pumping unit in adjacent rack. No raised floor or external water piping needed. Deploys into existing air-cooled halls. Meta fleet-wide AALC rollout confirmed early 2025. Gallatin is active Meta campus. Source: DCF 2024, EnkiAI 2025, OCP presentation.

facility_name: Meta Prineville Data Center — Prineville, OR (AALC Retrofit)
opportunity_summary: Meta's original data center campus (first outside leased space). 4.6M sq ft across ~11 buildings with ongoing expansion ($800M+ investment from 2024). By early 2025, AALC deployed across Meta fleet for AI server cooling. Prineville uses evaporative cooling for facility-level, but AI racks get AALC cold plates for direct-to-chip liquid cooling.
notes: Prineville = Meta's first owned DC (2011). Massive campus. AALC retrofitted into existing air-cooled data halls for AI servers. Meta roadmap: AALC now, next-gen DCs with full facility water plant in 2026+. Source: DCF 2024, DPR Construction.

facility_name: Meta Lulea Data Center — Lulea, Sweden (AALC Retrofit)
opportunity_summary: Meta's first DC outside US (2013). PUE 1.09 via free-air cooling (Arctic climate). 100% hydropower. By early 2025, Meta is deploying AALC across fleet for AI workloads. Lulea's sub-zero climate + AALC creates extremely efficient hybrid. Meta LLaMA training nodes all shifted to liquid cooling.
notes: Lulea uses 100% outdoor air for facility cooling -- no chillers needed. PUE 1.09. AALC adds direct-to-chip liquid cooling for AI racks within existing air-cooled halls. Meta confirmed fleet-wide AALC rollout early 2025. Source: EnkiAI 2025, LinkedIn analysis.

facility_name: Meta Eagle Mountain Data Center — Eagle Mountain, UT (AALC Retrofit)
opportunity_summary: Major Meta campus in Utah with 2022 mega expansion adding ~2M sq ft. By early 2025, AALC deployed across Meta fleet for AI server cooling. AALC achieves 120kW/rack in 20kW air-cooled halls. Utah's dry climate well-suited for evaporative facility cooling with AALC for AI racks.
notes: Eagle Mountain expanded significantly in 2022+. Meta's fleet-wide AALC rollout covers this site. AALC = no raised floor, no external water piping for liquid cooling. Source: DCF 2024, Baxtel.

facility_name: Meta Montgomery AI Data Center — Montgomery, AL
opportunity_summary: Broke ground 2024, operational by 2026. Part of Meta's 7 newest DC communities. Meta's next-gen AI-optimized DCs (2026 onward) include facility water plant with full DTC liquid cooling. Montgomery as a new-build campus is likely to incorporate next-gen cooling from the start.
notes: One of Meta's newest sites. Next-gen AI-optimized DC design slated for 2026 includes facility-level liquid cooling infrastructure. Source: Meta Newsroom Nov 2025, EnkiAI 2025.

facility_name: Meta Jeffersonville AI Data Center — Jeffersonville, IN
opportunity_summary: Broke ground 2024, operational by 2026. Part of Meta's 7 newest DC communities. Meta's next-gen AI-optimized DCs with facility water plant and DTC liquid cooling. New-build site likely incorporates next-gen cooling design.
notes: One of Meta's newest sites. Meta confirmed fleet-wide shift to liquid cooling for AI. New builds get full DTC infrastructure. Source: Meta Newsroom Nov 2025, EnkiAI 2025.

facility_name: Meta Rosemount AI Data Center — Rosemount, MN
opportunity_summary: Broke ground 2024, operational by 2026. Part of Meta's 7 newest DC communities. Meta's next-gen AI-optimized DCs with facility water plant and DTC liquid cooling. Minnesota cold climate = ideal for hybrid air + liquid cooling.
notes: New build 2024. Meta's fleet-wide DTC liquid cooling shift. Cold climate site favorable for cooling efficiency. Source: Meta Newsroom Nov 2025, EnkiAI 2025.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate Abilene Texas AI Campus
opportunity_summary: Flagship Stargate site. 8 buildings across 1,000 acres. Two buildings operational since Sep 2025 with NVIDIA GB200 Blackwell racks. Remaining 6 buildings by mid-2026 = 1.2GW total. Direct-to-chip closed-loop non-evaporative cooling. Liquid coolant piped directly onto GPU cold plates in sealed circuit. Winter 2026 weather event disrupted liquid cooling -- lessons learned.
notes: Operational Sep 2025. Closed-loop DTC liquid cooling confirmed by Oracle blog Feb 2026. Winter disruption exposed liquid cooling resilience challenges in extreme cold. 600MW expansion scrapped, but larger 4.5GW agreement on track. Source: CNBC Sep 2025, Oracle Blog Feb 2026, Tom's Hardware.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate Saline Township Michigan AI Campus
opportunity_summary: 1GW campus. Three 550,000 sq ft single-story buildings on 250 acres. Closed-loop cooling confirmed. Construction start early 2026, pending MI Public Service Commission approval. Walbridge as GC. DTE Energy + battery storage for power.
notes: NOTE: We already have this in new-us-emerging-w3.json as 'Oracle/Stargate The Barn'. This entry adds closed-loop cooling confirmation from Oracle Feb 2026 blog. Source: PR Newswire Oct 2025, Oracle Blog Feb 2026.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate Vantage Wisconsin AI Campus
opportunity_summary: $15B investment. Four data centers on 674 acres. 902MW IT capacity. Closed-loop cooling and zero-emission energy capacity confirmed. Vantage as development partner. Oracle blog confirms direct-to-chip closed-loop non-evaporative cooling across all Stargate sites.
notes: NOTE: We already have this in new-us-mid-w2.json. This adds Oracle blog confirmation of closed-loop DTC cooling and $15B figure. Source: DCK 2026, Oracle Blog Feb 2026.

facility_name: Oracle/Stargate Dona Ana County New Mexico AI Campus
opportunity_summary: One of five new Stargate sites announced Sep 2025. Oracle confirms direct-to-chip closed-loop non-evaporative cooling in all AI data centers including New Mexico. Desert climate makes zero-water cooling critical.
notes: Announced Sep 2025. Oracle blog Feb 2026 explicitly names New Mexico as one of the states with closed-loop cooling. Source: DCD Sep 2025, Oracle Blog Feb 2026.

facility_name: Oracle OCI Liquid-Cooled NVIDIA GB200 Supercluster
opportunity_summary: OCI now offers liquid-cooled NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 systems scaling to 131,072 Blackwell GPUs on OCI Supercluster. Direct-to-chip cold plate liquid cooling for all GB200 systems. 65,000+ GPU supercluster generally available. Oracle deployed first wave of liquid-cooled GB200 NVL72 racks. Dry coolers + closed-loop chillers with zero water wastage.
notes: Specific OCI region locations for liquid-cooled GB200 not individually disclosed. Oracle SVP acknowledged 'most data centres are not ready for liquid cooling.' Oracle scaling liquid cooling across OCI. Source: NVIDIA Blog, Oracle Blog.

facility_name: Alibaba Cloud Hangzhou Super Data Center (Immersion Cooling)
opportunity_summary: World's largest liquid-cooled cluster. Tens of thousands of liquid-cooled servers submerged in specialized insulating coolant. PUE 1.09 (world's lowest at time of deployment). Single-phase immersion cooling reduces energy consumption by 70%+ vs air-cooled. One of Alibaba's five Super Data Centers.
notes: NOTE: We already have Alibaba Renhe IDC in existing data. Hangzhou Super DC is the flagship immersion cooling site, separate from Renhe. World's largest liquid-cooled cluster claim. PUE 1.09 confirmed by Alibaba blog. Source: Alibaba Cloud Blog.

facility_name: Alibaba Cloud Heyuan Super Data Center
opportunity_summary: One of Alibaba's five Super Data Centers (with Hangzhou, Zhangbei, Ulanqab, Nantong). Part of the world's largest liquid-cooled data center network. Serves Double 11 (Singles Day) computing peaks. Alibaba's immersion cooling technology deployed across Super DC fleet.
notes: Heyuan in Guangdong province. Part of Alibaba's five Super DC network that collectively form the world's largest liquid-cooled DC cluster. Specific immersion cooling details for Heyuan not individually disclosed but confirmed as part of fleet. Source: Alibaba Cloud Blog.

facility_name: Alibaba Cloud Nantong Super Data Center
opportunity_summary: One of Alibaba's five Super Data Centers. Part of the world's largest liquid-cooled DC network. Nantong is in Jiangsu province near Shanghai. Alibaba committed to promoting liquid cooling throughout China and internationally.
notes: Part of five Super DC fleet. Alibaba uses UPS optimization at Nantong + liquid cooling. Near Shanghai economic zone. Source: Alibaba Cloud Blog.

facility_name: ByteDance Huailai Data Center — Hebei, China
opportunity_summary: ByteDance self-built DC in Huailai, Hebei Province (near Beijing). Supports Doubao AI model training. Kilocalorie-level GPU clusters requiring liquid cooling as standard (not optional). Guangzhou Goaland Energy Conservation provides liquid cooling solutions to ByteDance. Cold plate = 95% of China's DC liquid cooling market. ByteDance was NVIDIA's largest Chinese customer in 2024.
notes: Huailai is a major DC cluster zone near Beijing. ByteDance operates as 'landlord' of own DCs. Liquid cooling confirmed via Goaland supplier relationship and industry reporting on ByteDance GPU clusters. Source: Tom's Hardware 2025, 36Kr.

facility_name: ByteDance Malaysia AI Data Center (Aolani Cloud / Blackwell)
opportunity_summary: $2.1B+ Malaysia investment. Working with Aolani Cloud to deploy ~500 NVIDIA Blackwell computing systems (~36,000 B200 chips) in Malaysia. Circumvents US export restrictions by deploying in third country. NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 racks require direct-to-chip liquid cooling. Johor is emerging as major APAC AI hub.
notes: Mar 2026 announcement. NVIDIA confirmed no objections to deal. 36,000 B200 chips = massive liquid cooling requirement. Blackwell architecture requires liquid cooling at rack level. Part of ByteDance's broader $2.1B Malaysia expansion. Source: Seoul Economic Daily Mar 2026, Tom's Hardware Mar 2026.

facility_name: ByteDance Thailand Data Center
opportunity_summary: $3.8B investment announced Jan 2025 by Thailand Board of Investment. Operations expected 2026. At this scale and for AI workloads, liquid cooling is extremely likely. Tropical climate = cooling is critical design constraint.
notes: Liquid cooling not individually confirmed but at $3.8B scale with AI workloads in tropical climate, advanced cooling is a near-certainty. Source: DataGarda Jan 2025.

facility_name: Apple Private Cloud Compute — US Data Centers (Multiple Sites)
opportunity_summary: Apple shipping Private Cloud Compute servers from Houston manufacturing facility to DCs across US. M5 chip-based architecture (skipping M3/M4 Ultra). Dedicated AI server chip mass production H2 2026. Apple M-series silicon has much lower TDP than NVIDIA GPUs, so liquid cooling may not be required. Maiden NC uses outside air + waterside economizer. No public disclosure of liquid cooling at Apple DCs.
notes: IMPORTANT: Apple has NOT publicly disclosed liquid cooling at any of its data centers. M-series silicon's low power profile may not require liquid cooling. Maiden uses outside air cooling. Apple's $600B US commitment includes Houston server factory + DC expansion. I could not verify liquid cooling from available sources. Source: Tom's Hardware Oct 2025, 9to5Mac Feb 2026.

facility_name: IBM Quantum Data Center — Ehningen, Germany
opportunity_summary: First IBM Quantum Data Center outside US. Opened Oct 2024. Two IBM Quantum Eagle-based systems + new Heron-based system. Cryogenic cooling to near-absolute-zero + water-cooled support infrastructure. IBM is pioneer of microfluidics and two-phase cooling R&D. Germany is key hub for IBM's water-cooled computing heritage (QPACE supercomputer). IBM now proliferating liquid cooling tech through partners (Microsoft, Google, AWS, Lenovo, AMD, Danfoss).
notes: Quantum DCs use cryogenic dilution refrigerators (15 millikelvin) not conventional liquid cooling. But IBM's broader liquid cooling IP (microfluidics, Corintis partnership with Microsoft) is significant for the DC market. IBM is technology enabler, not competitor to Vertiv etc. Source: IBM Newsroom Oct 2024, EnkiAI 2025.

facility_name: IBM Quantum Computation Center — Poughkeepsie, NY
opportunity_summary: IBM's primary Quantum Computation Center. Houses multiple quantum systems. Project Goldeneye large-scale cryogenic concept system being developed here for next-gen quantum DC cooling needs. IBM's liquid cooling heritage goes back decades (mainframes, POWER systems). Poughkeepsie is IBM's historic computing manufacturing hub.
notes: Cryogenic cooling for quantum + conventional liquid cooling for supporting HPC infrastructure. Project Goldeneye exploring large-scale cryogenic systems for future quantum DCs. Source: IBM Quantum Blog.
